content.problemStatement:
You have a sequence aaa with length NNN created by removing some elements (possibly zero) from a permutation of numbers (1,2,‚Ä¶,N)(1, 2, \dots, N)(1,2,‚Ä¶,N). When an element is removed, the length of the sequence doesn't change, but there is an empty spot left where the removed element was. You also have an integer KKK.

Let's call a permutation p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \dots, p_Np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã good if:


it is possible replace empty spots in aaa by numbers in such a way that we obtain the permutation ppp
the number of positions iii (1<i‚â§N1 \lt i \le N1<i‚â§N) such that pi>pi‚àí1p_i \gt p_{i-1}pi‚Äã>pi‚àí1‚Äã is equal to KKK
Your task is to find the number of good permutations.
constraints: each integer between 111 and NNN inclusive appears in aaa at most once
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äã. Each element of this sequence is either 000 (indicating an empty spot previously occupied by a removed element) or an integer between 111 and NNN inclusive.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of good permutations.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The two possible good permutations are (2,3,1)(2,3,1)(2,3,1) and (2,1,3)(2,1,3)(2,1,3).
